Gated content, it works a little differently, so it's still a Facebook lead ad. The advertiser though, when creating the form, uploads that file to Facebook, then when the lead completes the

Alright, so how do we set up gated content, alright, so I'm gonna assume that you've not, maybe haven't set up Facebook lead ads before, so the campaign objective in ads manager needs to be leads, assuming you've got ODACs, if you don't, the old method was lead generation would need to be your campaign objective, then within the ad set for conversion location, you need to select instant forms, and you create your ad as you normally would, and you'll need to create a new form, alright, and even creating that form, we don't need to go into details there, most of it is gonna stay the same as you normally would have set it up, alright, but the final step is the key here, there's a final step that's message for leads, and this is basically the screen that normally you would give them the thank you message, hey thanks for submitting your info, check your email in a moment, and it's gonna be sent to you, in the meantime you can click this button, go to our website, and read such and such, that's normally the way that final screen would be used, but in that section when you're setting it up, you can select a CTA button, previously the options were view website, or call business, that's a whole other topic where I can get there, the new option is view file, right, so now we want to select view file, and then you can then upload a PDF, JPEG, or PNG file, so image files or PDF, they can't be more than 10 megabytes, and if they're an image file, they have to be at least 1080 pixels, I believe, so that way when someone subscribes, when they get to the end of that form, it's gonna say hey you've unlocked this free, this content, and the CTA button will say whatever you want to say, it can say download now, you have instructions in there, and it'll automatically download to their phone, phone, or even desktop, because they can run there as well, so that's how you set this up. ## [06:07 - 09:11]

you can click in ads manager in the ads tab, that will allow you to download your leads, but that's related to the people who saw your ads paid, your ad can be shared as well, so there might be some organic people who opted in, you'll want that full list, so those leads expire in 90 days, so you want to access them in your lead center in your business suite, you'll then need to download those leads before they expire obviously, now the way I've always done it is I automatically sync my leads to my CRM, I use keep, doesn't matter what you use, and we'll get to the benefits of this in a moment, but it kind of not eliminates lessons the requirement that you really have to sync these things, right, because someone subscribes this thing, they don't want to wait a day for you to see they subscribed, and then export your list to your CRM and all that kind of stuff, right, but now with gated content they can automatically get access to it, great, so there's no requirement to automatically sync to your CRM, but you probably still may want to if you can, and if you did sync it, you'd use a third-party tool to do that, I use Zapier, use whatever you want, so that's just a quick tip on managing leads, cheers!
